diagrams.title: Access Matrix Model
diagrams.description: Conceptual model showing subjects (rows) and objects (columns) with permitted operations in cells
elements: Example: User1 can Read/Write File1 but only Read File2
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the abstract model for protection in operating systems

diagrams.title: Unix File Permissions 9-Bit Pattern
diagrams.description: Visual representation of three sets of permissions (owner, group, others) each with read, write, execute bits
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how Unix file permissions are structured and calculated

diagrams.title: ACL vs Capability List Implementation
diagrams.description: Two different approaches to implementing the access matrix: file-centric (ACL) vs user-centric (Capability)
elements: Capability List: UserA -> [File1: R/W, File2: R/W/X]
diagrams.purpose: Understanding practical implementations of protection mechanisms
